name: Binary Padding - Linux
notes:
Converted from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Igor Fits, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Binary Padding - Linux
SIGMA Description: Adversaries may use binary padding to add junk data and change the on-disk representation of malware.
This rule detect using dd and truncate to add a junk data to file.


False positives:
- Unknown

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%truncate%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%-s%') OR ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%dd%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%if=%') AND (NOT(LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%of=%')))) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Binary Padding - Linux on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when the event matches devicetype=11 AND LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%execve%' AND ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%truncate%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%-s%') OR ((LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%dd%' OR LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%if=%') AND (NOT(LOWER(UTF8(payload)) LIKE '%of=%')))) AQL filter query
